summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute code at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the tarball root containing GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)`) at line 6 inside the targets/sources configuration. npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present (even without an explicit install/postinstall lifecycle script), and GYP evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell command during its configure phase. This causes the embedded command to execute on the installer's machine as part of a normal `npm install`, functionally equivalent to a postinstall hook. A backstage plugin published under the @immobiliarelabs scope has no documented need to build a native addon, and using binding.gyp command-expansion to run shell at install time is a known supply-chain attack pattern rather than legitimate native-extension build configuration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
